cards.back: A scheduling algorithm where the CPU executes processes in the order they arrive
A scheduling algorithm where the CPU executes processes based on their priority
A scheduling algorithm where each process is given a time slice (quantum) to execute before the CPU switches to the next process
The process of switching from one process to another, involving saving the current state of the process and loading the state of the new process
A portion of code within a process that requires exclusive access to a shared resource, and multiple threads cannot execute within the same critical section simultaneously
